stories.title: Fix Stories tab empty state and pull-to-refresh
stories.description: On first launch, no stories appear and pull-to-refresh doesn't work because .refreshable is only on the storyList ScrollView — the emptyView and loadingView have no ScrollView or .refreshable modifier. Users see 'Pull down to refresh' text but can't actually pull. Fix the empty/offline states to support pull-to-refresh, and ensure the initial fetch works reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty state view is wrapped in a ScrollView with .refreshable modifier so users can pull to refresh
Offline empty state also supports pull-to-refresh (shows offline toast if no connection)
Initial .task fetch errors are not silently swallowed — retry logic or user-visible feedback exists
Stories appear on first launch when network is available
Code compiles successfully
stories.technicalNotes: The root issue is in StoriesView.swift. The storyList (line 52-101) has .refreshable but emptyView (line 118-131) and offlineEmptyView (line 135-148) are plain VStacks with no ScrollView. Wrap the empty states in ScrollView with .refreshable to enable pull-to-refresh. The refresh() function's catch block (line 184-186) silently swallows errors — at minimum log them. Also consider: the 5-minute cooldown (DataService.swift line 6) could block retries if the first attempt failed. The force parameter already bypasses this when called from .refreshable.

stories.title: Fix Whitepaper and Privacy Policy links in Settings
stories.description: The Whitepaper link in Settings points to https://jtfnews.org (homepage) instead of the actual whitepaper page. The Privacy Policy link also points to the homepage, and https://jtfnews.org/privacy returns 404. Fix the whitepaper URL and create an in-app Privacy Policy view since no web page exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Whitepaper & Methodology link opens https://jtfnews.org/whitepaper.html (confirmed to return 200 OK)
Privacy Policy navigates to a new in-app PrivacyPolicyView instead of opening a broken web link
PrivacyPolicyView displays the app's privacy policy: zero data collection, no analytics, no tracking, no accounts, no crash reporting SDKs
Privacy policy view follows the app's dark theme and calm design
Code compiles successfully
stories.technicalNotes: In SettingsView.swift line 109: change URL to 'https://jtfnews.org/whitepaper.html'. Line 111: replace the Link with a NavigationLink to a new PrivacyPolicyView. Create Views/Settings/PrivacyPolicyView.swift with a ScrollView containing the privacy policy text. The policy should state: no data collected, no analytics SDKs, no crash reporting, no user accounts, no device fingerprinting, data stored only on-device via SwiftData, network requests only to jtfnews.org. Don't forget to add the new file to project.pbxproj (4 places: PBXBuildFile, PBXFileReference, PBXGroup children for Settings group, PBXSourcesBuildPhase).

stories.title: Fix YouTube video player Error 153 and Watch on YouTube link
stories.description: The YouTube embed shows 'Error 153 — Video player configuration error'. This is because the WKWebView loads the embed URL directly instead of using an HTML page with a proper YouTube iframe. Also, the 'Watch video on YouTube' link rendered by YouTube's error overlay does nothing because WKWebView has no navigation delegate to handle external URLs. Fix both issues.
acceptanceCriteria: YouTube video plays successfully in the Digest tab video mode without Error 153
Video embed uses an HTML template with YouTube iframe API instead of loading embed URL directly
Tapping 'Watch video on YouTube' or any YouTube link opens Safari/YouTube app
WKNavigationDelegate handles navigation actions — allows YouTube embed frames, opens external URLs in Safari
A manual 'Watch on YouTube' button/link is added below the player for accessibility
Video player still respects inline playback and dark background styling
Code compiles successfully
stories.technicalNotes: In YouTubePlayerView.swift: instead of webView.load(URLRequest(url:embedURL)), load an HTML string with an iframe: '<iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/VIDEO_ID?playsinline=1&rel=0&modestbranding=1" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen allow="autoplay; encrypted-media"></iframe>' wrapped in a full HTML page with dark background. Add a Coordinator as WKNavigationDelegate that intercepts navigation actions — if the URL is not a youtube.com/embed URL, open it with UIApplication.shared.open(). In DigestView.swift: add a 'Watch on YouTube' Link below the YouTubePlayerView that opens the full youtube URL (stored in youtubeURL state). The youtubeURL from monitor.json is: 'https://youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID'.

stories.title: Format Archive day view with parsed story cards
stories.description: Archive day data displays as raw pipe-delimited text, making it unreadable. The format is: timestamp|sources|ratings|urls|audio_file|fact_text (with lines starting with # being comments). Parse this format and display each story as a formatted card matching the app's design language.
acceptanceCriteria: Archive day content is parsed from pipe-delimited format into structured story entries
Each archived story displays: fact text (prominent), source names, source ratings, and relative/formatted date
Comment lines (starting with #) are not displayed as stories but date header info is shown
Stories marked [CORRECTED] in their URLs field show a subtle correction indicator
Formatted view follows the app's dark theme with muted colors and generous whitespace
If parsing fails for a line, it degrades gracefully (skip or show raw text for that line only)
Code compiles successfully
stories.technicalNotes: The archive format per line is: 'timestamp|sources|ratings|urls|audio_file|fact_text'. Example: '2026-04-04T00:05:52.369633+00:00|NPR,BBC News|4.8 (74/155),5.0 (165/327)|https://www.npr.org/...,https://www.bbc.com/...|efb91719f35f.mp3|President Trump seeks $1.5 trillion...'. Lines starting with '#' are header comments (date, generation info). Create a parser struct (e.g. ArchivedStoryParser) that splits by '|' and returns an array of parsed entries. Update ArchiveView.swift to replace the raw Text(text) display (line 72-77) with a LazyVStack of formatted ArchivedStoryCard views. Each card should show the fact text prominently, source names as badges, ratings next to sources, and the timestamp formatted as a time (e.g. '12:05 AM'). Check for '[CORRECTED]' prefix in the URLs field to detect corrections. Create the parser and card view — either inline in ArchiveView or as new files. Don't forget project.pbxproj if creating new files.
